_comment_instructions: This policy is for the hd-admin deployment IAM user ONLY.
Replace BUCKET_NAME with your S3 bucket name (e.g. antiguedad-rds-prod).
Replace with a comma-separated list any additional client prefixes as the platform grows.
Remove all '_comment_*' keys before pasting into AWS.

_comment_1: This is the global admin namespace: client_registry, username_index,

_comment_2: Read-only access to ALL client prefixes.
Required for: client-jump context (hopdesk staff browsing client data),
global audit log reads (can_view_global_audit), user limit checks.
Write access to client prefixes is granted separately — only for specific
admin operations (user deactivation, limit enforcement) via the app.
Note: this grants read on ALL prefixes under the bucket. With per-client IAM
hardening this would be replaced by explicit prefix list + STS role assumption.

_comment_3: Write access to specific client objects that hd-admin needs to modify:
- usuarios.rds: user deactivation during limit enforcement
- app_audit.rds: dual audit log writes (hd-admin + client side)
We deliberately do NOT grant blanket PutObject on all client prefixes here.
The resource ARNs below enumerate specific file patterns.
Add additional patterns if the admin flow requires writing other files.

_comment_4: Allow ListBucket scoped to hd-admin/ and any client prefix.
The hd-admin app needs to list chunk files (app_audit_001.rds, etc.).

_comment_5: Allow versioning-related actions needed by emergency_rewind.R.
These are bucket-level actions, not object-level.
ListBucketVersions: enumerating object versions for targeted key restore.
